g.text: Where Overlaat fits in a self-hosted LLM stack
A fair-queue + honest-accounting ring around an off-the-shelf serving stack.
• cost-weighted priority queue over one shared GPU budget
• admission, not rejection — overflow WAITS, never a 429
• emits exactly ONE lifecycle event per request
Loopback-only, so the proxy is the only door.
gateway fans to two serving kinds (off-the-shelf):
A GROUP MUTEX for big models
that can't co-reside: only ONE is
resident at a time. A sibling
calls are recorded too — not just
the ones that ran to completion.
The gateway routes; the swap layer loads and evicts.
